_README: Typed port manifest per the Connection Standard (CONNECTORS.md). The composer wires two ports iff their `type` matches a name in the shared vocabulary (types.json). `name` is the literal wiring address: an input is read under state[name]; an output is written under output[name].

inputs.name: login
inputs.desc: Submitted email + plaintext code + the orchestrator-fetched outstanding stored code record + now.

outputs.name: session
outputs.desc: Issued authenticated session descriptor on accept; null on reject.

_proposed_types_note: LoginAttempt and Session are not yet in the orchestrator's types.json. Their proposed schemas are in types-proposed.json in this repo and described in the PR body for vocabulary review (CONNECTORS.md: new types are reviewed, not minted freely).

_checked.corpus_rationale: Not a duplicate. organ-auth is the sole organ validating a first-party stored emailed one-time login code -> issuing a Session. The other auth-named organs cover fundamentally different mechanisms: organ-ms-auth routes the Microsoft OAuth 2.0 authorization-code flow (build_auth_url/exchange_code/refresh_token) for MS Graph/Teams; organ-gmail-credentials-credentials and organ-gmail-inbox-auth route Gmail OAuth credential freshness/token-refresh. None verify a one-time login code. The split mirrors discovery-engine's own services: email_auth.py (this organ's source) vs ms_auth.py / gmail_credentials.
_checked.oss_rationale: No permissive-OSS adopt-candidate. The leading permissive lib, PyOTP (MIT), implements RFC-4226 HOTP / RFC-6238 TOTP — time/counter-derived authenticator-app codes, a DIFFERENT mechanism from this organ's stored-random emailed code; adopting it would change product behaviour, not cut maintenance. Full OSS auth frameworks (Authlib, SuperTokens, Ory Kratos, Keycloak, Authentik, Flask-Login, LinOTP) are stateful, side-effecting systems that own exactly the impure edges (DB rows, sessions, SMTP, signed cookies) this pure decider deliberately excludes — adopting one inverts the organ architecture. The verification itself is ~30 lines of stdlib hmac.compare_digest over a salted hashlib hash plus expiry/single-use/attempt-budget checks (already best-practice, fail-safe reject); the stdlib is the correct dependency.
